question.text: Priya, a resident individual, has a total income of Rs. 75,00,000 in FY 2025-26 under the old tax regime. What rate of surcharge is applicable on her income tax?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Surcharge rates on income tax (for income other than STCG u/s 111A or LTCG u/s 112/112A): Up to Rs. 50L = Nil; Rs. 50L to Rs. 1 Cr = 10%; Rs. 1 Cr to Rs. 2 Cr = 15%; Rs. 2 Cr to Rs. 5 Cr = 25%; Above Rs. 5 Cr = 37%. Since Priya's income of Rs. 75L falls in the Rs. 50L-1Cr bracket, the surcharge rate is 10%. **The Trap:** 15% applies to income between Rs. 1 Cr and Rs. 2 Cr — candidates may round up Rs. 75L towards the next bracket. 25% applies to Rs. 2-5 Cr range. Nil applies only up to Rs. 50L. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A also covers surcharge computation for HNI advisory scenarios. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Surcharge significantly increases the effective tax burden for HNI clients — always factor it in when projecting post-tax returns.
